title: Home Sweet Homediddly Dum Doodily

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature exclamation opening the episode
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated grunt that defines Homer's character
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, highly quotable

jokes.text: Here's your toast, Maggie. I melba-fied it myself.
jokes.setup: Marge making toast for baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: The silly made-up verb 'melba-fied' for making melba toast
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that fits Marge's nurturing character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It was no trouble. The hoboes at the dump were very helpful. Except one man, who seemed to have mental problems.
jokes.setup: Marge got newspapers for Lisa's project
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning getting help from hoboes at a dump, with understated concern about one's mental state
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons dark humor - Marge's innocent delivery of disturbing content

jokes.text: And don't put signs on your sister!
jokes.setup: Marge giving instructions for school photo day
jokes.punchline: Implies Bart has been putting signs on Lisa, referenced casually as routine misbehavior
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - Marge dealing with Bart's typical pranks

jokes.text: Marge! There's a spider near my car keys!
jokes.setup: Homer calling for help with a domestic situation
jokes.punchline: The grown man is terrified of a spider just being 'near' his keys
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cowardice - contradicts his earlier statement about Marge fussing too much

jokes.text: You did the right thing by telling me.
jokes.setup: Homer called for help with the spider
jokes.punchline: Marge treats his cowardly overreaction as reasonable and mature
jokes.explanation: Marge's enabling of Homer's ridiculous behavior delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: No, we shan't. Yoicks!
jokes.setup: Car salesman trying to discuss the car
jokes.punchline: Homer uses pretentious language 'shan't' then an archaic hunting cry 'Yoicks!' to end discussion
jokes.explanation: Homer's bizarre vocabulary choices - mixing formality with nonsense

jokes.text: If anyone deserves to be wrapped up in seaweed and buried in mud, it's you.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Marge she deserves a spa day
jokes.punchline: Describes spa treatments in the most unappetizing, funeral-like terms possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - Homer's attempt at romantic persuasion sounds like threatening burial

jokes.text: Lice? How on earth does a boy get head lice in this day and age?
jokes.setup: Nurse discovers Bart has head lice
jokes.punchline: Treats lice as an antiquated problem despite it being common in schools
jokes.explanation: Mild irony about a common childhood problem being treated as shocking

jokes.text: We bought a wicker basket from Trader Pete's, and he was passed out inside.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he got lice
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions finding an unconscious person in a shopping basket
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdity - treating a bizarre situation as mundane

jokes.text: Hey, how come I get lice and nothing happens to Milhouse? So cold. So very, very cold.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about unfairness while being deloused
jokes.punchline: Milhouse shivering from the delousing treatment, undermining Bart's complaint
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with Milhouse clearly suffering, contradicting Bart's perception

jokes.text: What kind of parents would permit such a lapse in 'scalpal' hygiene?
jokes.setup: Skinner being pompous about the lice situation
jokes.punchline: Makes up the pretentious term 'scalpal hygiene' instead of saying scalp hygiene
jokes.explanation: Skinner's characteristic pompous vocabulary - making up medical-sounding terms

jokes.text: She could be rife with them 'boogs' too.
jokes.setup: Willie suggesting Lisa should be checked for lice
jokes.punchline: His crude pronunciation 'boogs' for bugs in his Scottish accent
jokes.explanation: Willie's characteristic mispronunciation - fits his rough character

jokes.text: Principal Skinner, I need some 'sooes.'
jokes.setup: Ralph approaching Skinner
jokes.punchline: His innocent mispronunciation of 'shoes' as 'sooes'
jokes.explanation: Ralph's typical childish mispronunciation - fits character but predictable

jokes.text: Sweet Georgia Brown! Something is rotten at the Simpson house!
jokes.setup: Skinner discovering the scope of the Simpson family problems
jokes.punchline: His old-fashioned exclamation 'Sweet Georgia Brown!' combined with Hamlet reference
jokes.explanation: Skinner's characteristic mix of outdated expressions and literary pretension

jokes.text: Oh, don't mind-a me. Look. I do it first.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about the mafia guy staring while they're in towels
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony helpfully removes his towel first to make them comfortable
jokes.explanation: Unexpected politeness from a mob boss in an awkward situation

jokes.text: Seems confused and dehydrated. Where's the baby? Well, that's her, ain't it?
jokes.setup: Social worker assessing the Simpson home
jokes.punchline: Grampa points to what's apparently a houseplant or object, mistaking it for baby Maggie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag - Grampa's confusion reaches absurd levels

jokes.text: Kids love that water. Oh, my lord! Stupid babies need the most attention.
jokes.setup: Grampa trying to explain baby care
jokes.punchline: Calls babies 'stupid' matter-of-factly while baby Maggie is presumably in danger
jokes.explanation: Grampa's inappropriate but quotable wisdom - dark humor about childcare

jokes.text: Squalid hellhole? Toilet paper hung in improper overhand fashion? Dogs mating on dining room table?
jokes.setup: Marge reading the child services report
jokes.punchline: Escalation from serious to trivial to absurd violations
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - from serious to petty to absurd complaints

jokes.text: Yea! Hey-diddly-ho! Welcome to your new home, neglect-a-rinos!
jokes.setup: Flanders greeting the Simpson children
jokes.punchline: His signature 'diddly' speech pattern applied to calling them 'neglect-a-rinos'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders characterization - cheerful delivery of insensitive content

jokes.text: Uh, please don't hug me. It sickens me.
jokes.setup: Flanders family showing affection to the kids
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest, brutal rejection of their kindness
jokes.explanation: Bart's characteristic bluntness creating uncomfortable moment

jokes.text: That was a baby tooth. It was loose!
jokes.setup: Implication that Lisa's tooth fell out from malnutrition
jokes.punchline: Lisa's desperate attempt to explain it as natural, suggesting it looked bad
jokes.explanation: Lisa's embarrassment about looking malnourished - mild cringe humor

jokes.text: Don't you worry, little girl. We'll get you some nice county dentures.
jokes.setup: Flanders responding to Lisa's lost tooth
jokes.punchline: Assumes child needs dentures, completely missing normal development
jokes.explanation: Absurd overreaction that shows Flanders' inexperience with children and bureaucratic thinking

jokes.text: Oh, bitch, bitch, bitch!
jokes.setup: Homer blaming Grampa for the situation
jokes.punchline: Grampa dismissively calling Homer's legitimate complaints 'bitching'
jokes.explanation: Grampa's dismissive attitude toward serious family crisis fits his character

jokes.text: Lousy house plant! You son of a- I'll teach you to- D'oh-You-
jokes.setup: Homer saying he can only take care of a house plant
jokes.punchline: Immediately attacks the plant, proving he can't even handle that responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy - Homer immediately proving his own incompetence

jokes.text: Hey, kids! Nachos, Flanders-style! That's cucumbers with cottage cheese!
jokes.setup: Flanders offering nachos to the kids
jokes.punchline: His 'nachos' are actually the most un-nacho-like food possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders characterization - wholesome misdirection of junk food

jokes.text: I used to let the boys watch My Three Sons, but it got 'em all worked up before bedtime.
jokes.setup: Flanders agreeing to let them watch TV
jokes.punchline: He considers the wholesome 'My Three Sons' too stimulating for children
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Flanders' extreme conservatism about entertainment

jokes.text: Daddy, what's the red stuff coming out of kitty's ears? Uh, uh, that's, uh, just raspberry jam.
jokes.setup: Children watching violent cartoon
jokes.punchline: Ned's desperate, unconvincing explanation of cartoon violence as innocent
jokes.explanation: Flanders struggling with his decision to allow violent content

jokes.text: Dad, should I poke Rod with a sharp thing like the mouse did?
jokes.setup: Todd also watching the violent cartoon
jokes.punchline: Wants to immediately imitate the cartoon violence on his brother
jokes.explanation: Escalates the cartoon violence concern - child immediately wants to replicate it on sibling

jokes.text: I can see Lisa. But it might be a starfish.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to spy on his kids through binoculars
jokes.punchline: His uncertain identification of his own daughter
jokes.explanation: Good Homer stupidity moment - failing at the simplest parental task of recognizing his child

jokes.text: The number you have dialed can no longer be reached from this phone you negligent monster.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to call his children
jokes.punchline: Phone system has been programmed to insult him personally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - even technology judges Homer's parenting

jokes.text: No, I don't care for children.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if social worker has family
jokes.punchline: Child welfare worker admitting she doesn't like children
jokes.explanation: Perfect institutional irony - person in charge of child welfare openly dislikes children

jokes.text: I'm probably the last guy in the world who should have kids. Uh- W- W-Well, wait- Wait. Can I start again?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince social worker he's a good father
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits he's unfit then panics and asks for a do-over
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-sabotaging honesty followed by desperate backtracking

jokes.text: And, uh, Margaret? Who? Lady, you got the wrong file. It's Maggie. Oh, Maggie. I got nothin' against Maggie.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to show he cares about his children
jokes.punchline: Gets his own baby's name wrong, then dismissively says he has 'nothing against' her
jokes.explanation: Homer's parental incompetence reaches peak absurdity - can't remember his baby's name

jokes.text: When she swallowed that quarter, he spent all day with her.
jokes.setup: Lisa noting when Homer last gave Maggie attention
jokes.punchline: It was only because she had a medical emergency, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Dark observation about Homer's parenting - only attentive during crises

jokes.text: Come on, you gloomy Gusses. Who's up for a big bowl on nonfat ice milk?
jokes.setup: Flanders trying to cheer up the kids
jokes.punchline: Offers the most depressing possible version of ice cream
jokes.explanation: Flanders' health-conscious approach making treats less appealing

jokes.text: Put it in the refrigerator, or, failing that, a cool, wet sack.
jokes.setup: Basic parenting advice about milk storage
jokes.punchline: Backup plan sounds medieval and ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Absurd practical advice - modern parenting class with primitive backup plan

jokes.text: Garbage in garbage can. Hmm. Makes sense.
jokes.setup: Basic parenting instruction about garbage disposal
jokes.punchline: Homer treats this elementary concept as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - basic hygiene presented as revelation

jokes.text: Garbage in garbage can. Hmm. Makes sense.
jokes.setup: Instructor teaching basic cleanliness
jokes.punchline: Homer treats obvious information like profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - genuinely enlightened by the most basic life advice

jokes.text: They say your folks can't pay the rent / So we're watching you by act of government
jokes.setup: Flanders singing a bedtime song
jokes.punchline: Cheerful song about government intervention due to parental negligence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - making child welfare removal into a sweet lullaby

jokes.text: Remember how Mom used to microwave our underwear on cold days?
jokes.setup: Lisa and Bart reminiscing about their parents
jokes.punchline: Marge's caring but unconventional parenting methods
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Marge's unconventional but loving care for her children

jokes.text: Or the way Dad used to call the radio station with fake traffic tips?
jokes.setup: Bart also reminiscing fondly
jokes.punchline: Homer's irresponsible but memorable behavior becomes nostalgic
jokes.explanation: Shows how Homer's ridiculous behavior becomes endearing family memories

jokes.text: Saxo-mo-phone
jokes.setup: Homer missing Lisa's saxophone playing
jokes.punchline: His characteristic mispronunciation of 'saxophone'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer callback - his signature mispronunciation showing genuine emotion

jokes.text: I wish I knew something about the baby I could miss now. You mean Maggie? That's it.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to express missing his children
jokes.punchline: Admits he knows so little about his baby he can't think of anything to miss
jokes.explanation: Dark honesty about Homer's disconnection from his own child

jokes.text: Why, you cotton-pickin'- Hey!
jokes.setup: Homer role-playing in parenting class
jokes.punchline: Starts to use his usual violent reaction then catches himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's moment of self-awareness interrupting his automatic response

jokes.text: Son, let's stop the fussin' and the feudin'. I love you, Pa! I love you, Cletis!
jokes.setup: Homer practicing conflict resolution in parenting class
jokes.punchline: Emotional breakthrough happens with hillbilly classmate instead of his actual child
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - Homer's parenting breakthrough happens with wrong person, shows his confusion

jokes.text: Artaxerxes! Righty-o!
jokes.setup: Bible trivia game with obscure Persian king question
jokes.punchline: Young child immediately knows ancient Persian king, Ned's cheerful approval
jokes.explanation: Absurd level of biblical knowledge expected from small children

jokes.text: The serpent of Reheboam? The well of Zohassadar? The bridal feast of Bethchedruharazzeb?
jokes.setup: Flanders frustrated that kids don't know bible trivia
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and made-up sounding biblical references
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of increasingly absurd biblical names that sound made up

jokes.text: Actually, you see- we were never baptized. Oh! Neddie? Neddie!
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why they don't know bible trivia
jokes.punchline: Maude's horrified reaction to unbaptized children
jokes.explanation: Maude's religious horror at unbaptized children fits character expectations

jokes.text: Marge, you tested positive for crack and P.C.P. Oh, my! Okay, the retest says you're clean. Sorry about the mistake.
jokes.setup: Everyone passed except Marge
jokes.punchline: Wholesome Marge tested positive for hard drugs, immediately corrected as lab error
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - innocent Marge failing drug test briefly before correction

jokes.text: The only thing I'm high on is love- love for my son and daughters. Yes, a little L.S.D. is all I need.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining she's not on drugs
jokes.punchline: Accidentally makes drug reference with 'L.S.D.' meaning 'love for son and daughters'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - Marge innocently creating perfect drug acronym while being sincere

jokes.text: Have you thought about one of the other major religions? They're all pretty much the same.
jokes.setup: Flanders panicking about unbaptized children
jokes.punchline: Minister casually dismisses religious differences as unimportant
jokes.explanation: Reverend's theological indifference contrasting with Ned's panic

jokes.text: Damn Flanders.
jokes.setup: Ned calling reverend about emergency baptism situation
jokes.punchline: Religious leader curses the overly religious parishioner
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - even the reverend finds Flanders' extreme religiosity annoying

jokes.text: Kids! We're good parents now! Get your asses out here!
jokes.setup: Homer announcing completion of parenting class
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates bad parenting by swearing at his children
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - Homer's graduation immediately undermined by his behavior

jokes.text: In the eyes of God, they'll be Flanderseseseses.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about the baptism
jokes.punchline: His confused attempt to pluralize 'Flanders' with excessive 'es' endings
jokes.explanation: Homer's grammatical confusion creating accidentally funny plural form

jokes.text: Daddily-doodily.
jokes.setup: Maggie speaking in the Flanders household
jokes.punchline: Baby's first word incorporates Flanders' speech pattern instead of normal 'daddy'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff - Maggie adopting Flanders' linguistic quirks as her first word

jokes.text: Oh, why do you hate my trains?
jokes.setup: Homer causing destruction during the rescue
jokes.punchline: Flanders' plaintive question about his model trains being destroyed
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy with Flanders' characteristic whining about property damage

jokes.text: Oh, Bartholomew, I feel like St. Augustine of Hippo after his conversion by Ambrose of Milan.
jokes.setup: Bart asking how Homer feels after being baptized
jokes.punchline: Homer suddenly speaks with sophisticated theological knowledge completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Absurd temporary character change - Homer briefly becoming theologically sophisticated

jokes.text: Oh, Bartholomew, I feel like St. Augustine of Hippo... after his conversion by Ambrose of Milan.
jokes.setup: Bart asking how Homer feels after being baptized
jokes.punchline: Homer suddenly speaks with scholarly religious knowledge
jokes.explanation: Absurd temporary character change - Homer briefly becomes religiously scholarly from baptism water

jokes.text: Ol' Painty-can Ned! I always knew he'd keep his old cans of paint.! Yeah.!
jokes.setup: Homer asking for 'dirt' on the Flanders
jokes.punchline: The only 'scandalous' thing is that Ned saves old paint cans, which Homer treats as vindication
jokes.explanation: Perfect anti-climactic ending - Homer's petty satisfaction with mundane 'dirt' on Flanders

tmdb_overview: When Homer and Marge are found to be unfit parents, the kids are placed with the Flanders family. Reluctant participants in the Flanderses' wholesome family activities, Bart and Lisa become the targets of an emergency baptism.
insight: Flanders parenting satire drives 60 jokes in 22 minutes, just shy of elite tier.
